No, we're looking backwards As well and learning from the lessons of the past Not just in this business from everything I've done But especially in this business because what you'll notice mark my words is one thing you will notice in life In business it's on all aspects of life but especially in business is that Things never work out on paper As they seem they don't work the things Things don't work out as they do on paper when you write out a business strategy a business plan I don't care how airtight it looks I don't care even if even if you've tested it and you get some traction when you start to scale You never know what's going to happen You just don't And if you don't stay on God, you're not absolutely vigilant Every single day scrutinizing what you're doing looking at every dollar going in every dollar going out What where's it? You know if you're not doing that and essentially reinventing yourself every single day on some level or improving yourself You'll be out of business before you know it Now i'll give you an example here One of the things that with bowing right I sat bowing down with Matt. I said, bow What do you think the overhead is? I don't want to give you these and he spat out a number Okay, and the number turned out to be one sixth of what our overhead actually is why because I knew he would do that Why because I made the same mistake when I was bowing's age my first business the it's so easy For overhead to get out of control See when you like teach entrepreneurship, right? I always divide it up into two Chunks right we always talk about this idea that to be a really successful businessman entrepreneur You need to do two things number one. You need to know how to fail elegantly In other words, how do you go to business and learn to be wrong? How do you launch ideas or divisions within a business and be wrong and not get destroyed? This is on some level the most important skill you can ever have I'm seriously without this one skill You'll never get to this part which is how do you succeed wildly? Meaning what do you do when the shit's working? And that's really the conversation You're gonna see us have today when we actually are now scaling our own business. We're growing up We're about to launch the certification program, right? We're about to blow it out to corporations. We're getting you know I've trained every person every corporation. I've shown this is like, where do I sign up? Every individual that's hurt us. How do I get this thing? Right? So we're like, all right And I know and and just see you understand the quality and you'll the second that you see it It's like nothing you've ever seen before Okay, not the point for today, but it's just it's just fucking ridiculous. All right. It's that effective and that great All right, and it took a fucking extra year an extra 500,000 fucking dollars. Okay, just so you know That's how much we've put into this thing on top of what was supposed to originally cost. All right so the idea though is that You have to know how to do this How do you go about? building your business Testing out new ideas and divisions and when they don't work out You don't get wrecked destroyed by which is what most people do that is the the mistake most people make is they fail miserably When you're failing elegantly you're failing quickly You're failing cheaply And if possibly you're failing with other people's money, but that's not always possible But the point is you're failing cheaply you're failing quickly and you're also Tracking your failure so you can maximize on the lessons In other words, you're objectively tracking what's right and what's wrong You're not getting caught up in the emotional aspects of not succeeding at something because once that happens You learn nothing when you end up doing you're saying Oh my god, I just i'm a shitty business. That business sucks or this sucks fuck Versus an objective businessman saying okay I really thought this would work. Let me analyze this like step by step move by move You know, you know point by point where did it work? Where did it work? Where do I pivot? Because the idea is is that when things don't work you need to learn how to pivot this auto flexibility being flexible as a business person, right? So when you're failing elegantly You're failing quickly meaning you're not Trying to be stronger than the market You're not trying to overpower your bad idea or flawed strategy with perseverance and work ethic those This is just so common for young entrepreneurs and older ones. But especially young ones is that you fall in love with your own Florida idea So everybody else sees that it's time to move on It's time to pivot time and that pivot could be a full-on pivot to something completely different or a minor turn of twist, right? But you're emotionally attached to your idea So you stay with it Far too long and you keep trying to like improve an idea that's never going to work Or never going to work in a way that is going to give you the outcome you want Yeah, Mike, you can improve it so you can make some money, but it's not going to be what you wanted That original vision that you had that massive success that used the brass ring at the end of the rainbow, right? The idea that you thought would get you there is just not going to That happens all the time guys. So before you actually open a fitness center, which is very cash flow intensive You'll go to a market and you tell them you're coming it allows you to do a couple things more engage interest and Get cash flow. So when you open your doors and when you start a project, you're not starting at zero You have a baseline, right? And you also adopt early. It's early adopts, right? Same it's very it's a it's the it's the system that's used by every real estate every builder When someone goes and builds a neighborhood, they don't wait and build all the houses then try to sell them What do they do? They build a model They draw a plans, right and they sell off the plans off the model You it would be impossible to make money It would be too cash flow intensive for someone to go the whole way and build all the homes then start selling them They start selling the homes before they're even built That's generally accepted policy same way in the health club business, right? Where you're going to go out there when you go into a market you'll do pre-sales So let's say your if your price is going to be you know, $14.99 a month in the gym business So you give them pre-sales you give them a 50% off for a 40% or some other benefit or an extra year So you give some benefit to sign them up now But in order to do that what do you need you need to have certain collateral? So it's not always so easy to fire people like that okay, so while Salaries is variable overhead. It's not fixed. It's somewhat fixed sometimes based on what it's supporting And what it connects to in your overall scheme. I have a ton of leads, but I do not use an automatic dialer I input the numbers one at a time Disappoint Yeah, and yeah, it's true. Yeah, discipline I always tell a story that um, but I was doing a training in a logistics company Private training and and I said you got to make 200 calls a day And they were like, what are you fucking talking? They like thought I was out of my mind, right? Well I the CEO stood behind you know, he embraced it and made them do it and the first week They did like 130 calls when they they were like like they thought they'd come back from the trenches of world one like Oh, they're bloody day from 100 Within the four or five days we're making 200 calls a day. It was like nothing. Okay It's the the key to increasing your call volume is really there's two things number one Don't prejudge the fucking lead that's in front of you just dial it That's the biggest thing that slows me down. You're good to lead Let me Google that person and find out more about them. It'll help me know it's fucking dial the phone That's the number the biggest thing that stops people. Okay, and two when you sit down to dial the phone don't do it and Set aside a 15 or 20 minute block set aside a two to three hour block Do it in blocks of time it you get on rolls. So don't try to just like, you know, oh, let me have a Let me do 15 minutes here. No, just you know, if you had to put your head down two hours, boom dial that fucking phone Those are the two quickest ways now Even if you don't load into a dialer, right? There's also you there's other there's sort of these bridge systems that aren't quite You know predictive dials or auto dials, but they do enhance most Files coming to csv form, right? And then and you can upload files into a program so you can just tap the space bar and there's many different ways like technically But the two I mentioned are more like tactically that what things you can do and a mindset you can have That's your biggest, you know pick up right there.
